description: UI scenario tests for mode switching and session persistence. Each scenario is executed against a running PolyPilot app using MauiDevFlow CDP commands. Scenarios assume the app starts in Persistent mode with sessions loaded.

scenarios.name: Sessions survive Persistent → Embedded → Persistent round trip

steps.note: Total sessions = visible items + collapsed counts. Compare with initial.

scenarios.name: Rapid Embedded↔Persistent switching preserves active-sessions.json
Connecting to unreachable server shows configuration needed
After failed Persistent connection, switching to Demo recovers
Sessions from active-sessions.json appear after app restart

steps.note: Sum all group counts (includes collapsed groups)

scenarios.name: CLI source switches between Built-in and System and persists
Mode selection persists to disk immediately without Save & Reconnect
Bug report button is visible in sidebar footer and opens inline form

steps.note: Verify bug report button exists in sidebar

scenarios.name: Refresh sessions button is visible in sidebar utility cluster and can be clicked

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('.sidebar-utility-cluster .sidebar-utility-btn'); if (!btn) return 'missing'; btn.click(); return 'clicked'; })()

steps.note: Verify toolbar remains available after refresh

scenarios.name: Create session model picker exposes GPT-5.4
scenarios.description: The create-session model selector should list GPT-5.4 from the Copilot CLI's discovered model list, without requiring PolyPilot to hardcode every supported model.

steps.note: GPT-5.4 should be visible in the picker

scenarios.name: Sessions recover from 'Thinking' state when persistent server dies mid-turn
scenarios.description: Validates that sessions stuck in IsProcessing=true recover automatically via the processing watchdog when the persistent server becomes unreachable.

steps.note: Wait for message to be sent and processing to start
Should show 'Thinking' or activity text while processing

steps.text: To fully test: kill the persistent server process while session is processing, then wait up to 2 minutes for the watchdog to detect inactivity and clear the stuck state (10 min if a tool is running). The session should show a system message: 'Session appears stuck — no response received.'

steps.note: Wait for watchdog timeout (120s when no tool running) + buffer. In manual testing, kill server during this wait.
Processing indicator should be gone after watchdog fires
System message about stuck session should appear in chat

scenarios.name: After relaunch, all previously-active sessions should be visible
scenarios.description: Validates that after relaunch.sh deploys a new build, session restore failures don't silently leave the app with 0 sessions. Covers the scenario where an old copilot server is running but individual session resumes fail.

steps.note: Wait for app to restart and restore sessions
App should be connected (Persistent or Embedded fallback)
Sessions should be visible after relaunch — not silently lost
No sessions should be stuck in processing state after relaunch

scenarios.name: Stop button clears message queue and processing status
scenarios.description: When user clicks Stop on an active session, the message queue is cleared so no more messages auto-send, and processing status fields (elapsed time, tool rounds) are reset.

steps.note: A session must be actively processing (IsProcessing=true)
Stop button should be visible for processing sessions
Session card should no longer show processing state after stop

scenarios.name: Processing sessions show elapsed time and tool round count
scenarios.description: When a session is processing, the UI shows a status indicator with elapsed time and tool round count. Before the first SDK event, it shows 'Waiting for first response'. Processing status fields are synced via bridge to mobile.

steps.note: A session must be actively processing
Active session should show processing card state
Processing status text (elapsed time / tool rounds / waiting) should be visible

scenarios.name: Stale sessions are NOT marked as processing on restore
scenarios.description: When a session's events.jsonl hasn't been modified in over 10 minutes, the session should not be restored with IsProcessing=true, even if the last event was an 'active' type. This prevents hours-old sessions from showing a permanent 'Thinking' indicator.

steps.note: Count sessions showing processing state — stale sessions should not be here

steps.text: Manual verification: check debug info for any sessions with IsProcessing=true where LastUpdatedAt is over 10 minutes old. Such sessions should have been detected as stale during restore.

scenarios.name: Saved Sessions section visible on desktop with session count

steps.note: Section header shows 'Saved Sessions (N)' with non-zero count
At least one persisted session item is rendered
Filter input is visible when expanded

scenarios.name: QR code and token have independent blur toggles

steps.note: Navigate to /settings page. Requires an active DevTunnel to see QR code.
QR code image is blurred by default (showQrCode=false)
Token value is also blurred by default (showToken=false)
Click the QR-specific reveal button (independent from token reveal)
QR code is revealed after clicking its own reveal button
Token remains blurred — QR and token toggles are independent

scenarios.name: Built-in CLI card is selectable when bundled CLI is missing but system CLI exists (fallback)

steps.note: Built-in card should NOT be disabled even if bundled CLI is missing (system fallback exists)
Click Built-in card to switch back
Verify Built-in is selected again (this was the bug: could not switch back)
Verify CliSource=0 (BuiltIn) persisted to disk after switching back

scenarios.name: Bang commands use platform-appropriate shell (cmd.exe on Windows, /bin/bash on Mac/Linux)

steps.note: Wait for shell command to execute
Shell output contains the echoed text, confirming the platform shell executed successfully

scenarios.name: Session contextual menu (⋯) closes when clicking outside, including main content area

steps.note: At least one session exists with a more-btn
Click in the main content area (outside sidebar)
Session menu closed after clicking outside in main content area

scenarios.name: Custom agents popup is clickable and inserts @agent-name into textarea

steps.note: Header should say 'Available Agents (click to use)'
Agent rows should have class 'agent-row' and be clickable

steps.script: (function(){ var row = document.querySelector('.agent-row'); var name = row?.getAttribute('data-agent'); row?.click(); return name; })()

steps.note: Popup should close after clicking an agent
Textarea should contain '@agent-name ' ready for the user to type their message

scenarios.name: Group contextual menu closes when clicking outside, including main content area

steps.note: At least one group exists with a more-btn
Click in the main content area
Group menu closed after clicking outside in main content area

scenarios.name: Cmd+, keyboard shortcut navigates to Settings page

steps.script: document.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', {key: ',', metaKey: true, bubbles: true})); true

scenarios.name: Statistics popup opens when clicking chart icon in sidebar header
Settings page category navigation scrolls to the selected section

steps.note: Click the UI category nav item
UI settings section exists with correct id

scenarios.name: Settings page no longer contains Statistics section

steps.note: Statistics is not a settings group

scenarios.name: Settings page UI section is rendered from SettingsRegistry descriptors

steps.note: UI group should contain data-driven setting rows
Search bar should be full-width in header

scenarios.name: Settings search filters individual setting rows

steps.note: Type font into search and trigger input event
steps.script: var el = document.getElementById('settings-search'); el.value = 'font'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input')); true

steps.note: Font size setting should still be visible after searching

scenarios.name: VS Code opens with Remote - Tunnels when in remote mode
scenarios.description: When connected to a remote PolyPilot server, clicking the VS Code context menu item should open VS Code with --folder-uri vscode-remote://tunnel+<serverMachineName>/<path> so it connects via the Remote - Tunnels extension instead of opening a local path.

steps.note: Prerequisites: App must be in Remote mode connected to a remote server
Right-click or long-press to open context menu
VS Code menu item is visible in the context menu

scenarios.name: Sidebar content has overflow:visible so info popover is not clipped

steps.note: .sidebar-content must have overflow:visible so the absolutely-positioned info popover can render outside sidebar bounds without being clipped

scenarios.name: Right-click on session item opens the ⋯ menu
scenarios.description: Right-clicking (contextual click) on a session in the left pane should open the same ⋯ menu that the ellipsis button opens, and suppress the browser default context menu.

steps.note: At least one session item exists in the sidebar

steps.script: var item = document.querySelector('.session-item'); var evt = new MouseEvent('contextmenu', {bubbles: true, cancelable: true}); var prevented = !item.dispatchEvent(evt); prevented
steps.note: contextmenu event is preventDefault'd on session items
Allow Blazor async event handler and re-render to complete before checking DOM
The session menu is visible after right-click

scenarios.name: Code blocks in settings use theme-aware colors, not Bootstrap red

steps.note: Settings page should render at least one .section-desc code element

steps.script: (() => { const codes = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.section-desc code')); const probe = document.createElement('span'); probe.style.color = 'var(--text-bright)'; probe.style.position = 'absolute'; probe.style.visibility = 'hidden'; document.body.appendChild(probe); const expected = getComputedStyle(probe).color; probe.remove(); return codes.every(el => getComputedStyle(el).color === expected); })()
steps.note: All .section-desc code elements should resolve to the theme's --text-bright color
Code elements in section-desc should have a visible border for contrast

scenarios.name: Automatic server restart when all sessions get stuck (auth token expiry)
scenarios.description: When the persistent server auth token expires, all sessions silently hang. After 2+ consecutive watchdog timeouts, the app should automatically restart the server and show a recovery notice.

steps.text: Simulate auth failure: send messages to 2+ sessions, wait for watchdog timeouts (~135s each). After the 2nd timeout, the app should attempt server recovery.

steps.note: After recovery, a notice should inform the user the server was restarted
